tools.title: American Epidemic Timeline
tools.summary: A source-backed teaching timeline of major U.S.-linked epidemics, outbreaks, and public-health turning points.
tools.evidence_model: Defensible outbreak ledger with source IDs, claim-level confidence categories, rights-checked archival visuals, visible uncertainty notes, and conservative public-health interpretation
tools.long_note: The timeline follows major epidemics, public-health turning points, and selected regional outbreaks where the evidence is strong enough to carry the story without overdiagnosis or fake precision.

tools.title: Pathogen Atlas
tools.summary: A source-backed teaching atlas about pathogen origins, reservoirs, transmission ecology, historical spread, and evidentiary uncertainty.
tools.evidence_model: Claim-level source IDs, origin confidence, evidence-type panels, and central source-registry validation
tools.long_note: The pathogen atlas runs as a dark digital exhibit with claim-level source IDs, confidence labels, evidence-type panels, and explicit uncertainty notes.

tools.title: Maritime Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A source-backed map-first digital exhibit on ships, ports, quarantine, naval warfare, captivity, trade, migration, and maritime disease ecology.
tools.evidence_model: Case modules with source IDs, route claim types, burden categories, confidence notes, uncertainty notes, mechanism filters, and guided-tour narrative
tools.long_note: This is the main public-history exhibit in the atlas family: an exploratory disease ecology surface with source-backed case modules, route confidence labels, and guided-tour and presentation modes for later video adaptation.

tools.title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
tools.long_note: A structured historical atlas page connecting colonial disease ecology, military movement, smallpox, inoculation, and public-health constraints around the founding period.

tools.title: Viking Health Atlas
tools.summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
tools.evidence_model: Map-based historical synthesis plus linked essays
tools.long_note: A Norse-world health and disease map connecting Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, pre-Columbian contact arguments, and epidemic uncertainty.
